assertions.title: Client secret creation returns ephemeral auth and effective session

reasons.observation: The mocked POST /v1/realtime/client_secrets request included expires_after.anchor=created_at, expires_after.seconds=60, session.type=realtime, and session.output_modalities=[text]. The decoded result exposed client_secret.value=ek_test_123, client_secret.expires_at=1740000000, session.type=realtime, and session.output_modalities=[text].

assertions.title: WebSocket connect builds the correct Realtime target and auth inputs

reasons.observation: prepare_ws_target produced wss://example.openai.invalid/v1/realtime?model=gpt-realtime-mini for model sessions and wss://example.openai.invalid/v1/realtime?call_id=call_123 for call joins, used Authorization: Bearer sk_server for server-key auth and Authorization: Bearer ek_test_secret / ek_consumer_secret for client-secret auth, preserved org/project/user-agent headers for consumer auth, rejected default auth without a server key as a configuration error, and rejected missing model/call_id as a validation error.

assertions.title: Session update round-trips effective GA session state

reasons.observation: The client sent a session.update event whose serialized session included instructions="" and output_modalities=[text]. The mock server responded with session.updated, and the decoded event preserved session.id=sess_123 and instructions="" as the effective session state.

assertions.title: Conversation item creation preserves ordering and server acknowledgment

reasons.observation: The client sent conversation.item.create with previous_item_id=root and a user message item. The server observed previous_item_id=root, returned conversation.item.created, and the decoded event preserved previous_item_id=root with item.id=item_123.

assertions.title: Realtime call creation helper chooses SDP vs multipart correctly

reasons.observation: The first POST /v1/realtime/calls used Content-Type: application/sdp, Accept: application/sdp, and body 'v=0\r\n' when only sdp was provided. The second POST /v1/realtime/calls used multipart with exactly two parts named sdp and session when session config was supplied, and both responses returned the SDP answer bytes unchanged.

assertions.title: Call control helpers hit the correct routes and guard path params

reasons.observation: The mocked helpers posted to /v1/realtime/calls/call_accept/accept, /call_hangup/hangup, /call_refer/refer, and /call_reject/reject. The accept request used Accept: */*, hangup sent an empty body, refer serialized target_uri=sip:agent@example.com, reject serialized status_code=486, and hangup with a blank call_id failed locally with ErrorKind::Validation before sending a request.

assertions.title: GA event names follow the current published surface, not stale beta aliases

reasons.observation: decode_server_event treated response.output_text.delta and response.output_text.done as first-class GA events, decoded output_audio_buffer.started as a first-class lifecycle event, and decoded the stale beta alias response.text.delta as RealtimeServerEvent::Unknown rather than a canonical GA event.

decode_server_event decoded response.output_audio.done into RealtimeServerEvent::OutputAudioDone with the expected ids and indices, confirming GA audio completion event support on the current surface.

assertions.title: Connection Bootstrap Surfaces Session Creation and Clean Shutdown

reasons.observation: Opening the mocked Realtime connection produced session_id=sess_123 before user traffic, next_event decoded session.created with session.id=sess_123, and connection.close() caused the server to receive a close frame while the client observed next_event() == None afterward without hanging.

summary: All 8 assigned realtime bootstrap assertions passed via the 3 specified mocked cargo validation commands; each command exited 0 and the named tests matched the expected bootstrap, call-route, and decode-contract behaviors.
